business.headline: From rising prices to rupee pressure: 5 key takeaways from RBI's annual reportIndia's economy remains strong, but challenges are growing. Slower growth, rising inflation risks and geopolitical tensions may test the economy in the coming months.

Why RBI says India's biggest threat lies beyond its bordersFor all the talk around India's strong economic growth, resilient consumers and healthy banks, the RBI's latest assessment contains a clear message: the biggest risks to the country's growth story are increasingly coming from outside its borders.

Under-construction or ready-to-move home: Which saves me more money in the long run?Buying a home is exciting, but one question often leaves buyers confused: should you go for a cheaper under-construction property or pay more for a ready-to-move home? Let's have a look.

Inspection Raj in the service of employers evading complianceIn the ninth part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines how reduced scrutiny in the name of "ease of doing business" may create space for wilful labour law violations.

Why Delta Corp share price crashed 16% todayDelta Corp shares slid 16% after the Supreme Court upheld the government's retrospective 28 per cent GST levy on online gaming companies. The ruling has intensified concerns over large tax demands and compliance costs across the sector.

8th Pay Commission: Pensioners seek option to choose between OPS, NPS, UPSEmployee and pensioner groups have urged the 8th Pay Commission to let government staff choose between OPS, NPS and UPS. The demand has put retirement security and clarity over post-retirement income at the centre of the wider pay panel discussions.

Wipro jumps 5%, lifts IT stocks as AI deal sparks sector rallyWipro shares: Wipro stock gained as much as 4.76% during the day to Rs 211.20, while its American Depositary Receipts (ADRs) listed in the US had jumped more than 18% overnight.

IT stocks lead gains as Sensex, Nifty open higher on easing oil pricesThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 98.09 points, or 0.13%, to 75,965.89 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 16.20 points, or 0.07%, to 23,923.35, as of 9:25 am.

SpaceX IPO: How Elon Musk's company is targeting a $1.8 trillion valuationSpaceX is discussing a valuation of about $1.8 trillion and a potential $75 billion raise ahead of its IPO. The filing shows the company is asking investors to see it as an AI infrastructure and space technology business.

Why India may be taking risky bet with $500 billion US dealMarco Rubio said in New Delhi that India had committed to buying $500 billion of American goods over five years. The remark has intensified scrutiny of whether the proposed arrangement offers India enough return.

The hidden salary leak Gen Z didn't notice until bank balances started shrinkingYoung salaried Indians are discovering that small app subscriptions are quietly eating into their monthly income. The online debate has sharpened focus on how auto-debits and convenience spending can distort budgeting.

Is E20 petrol quietly increasing your monthly fuel bill? 5 in 10 owners say yesA LocalCircles survey says many owners of older petrol vehicles have seen lower mileage after the E20 rollout. The findings sharpen consumer concerns over fuel costs, repairs and the shift towards higher ethanol blends.

Remote work is back in focus: Will it redefine how India chooses homes?Flexible work is again influencing how Indian homebuyers assess where to live. The shift could move demand beyond office hubs towards space, practicality and quality of life.

8th Pay Commission: Pension choice for government employees in coming monthsAt present, most central government employees recruited after January 1, 2004, fall under the National Pension System, which is contribution-based and linked to market performance.

Is RBI really supposed to defend the rupee amid ongoing crisis?Handcrafted ByOur Editors Every time the rupee falls, panic erupts across television studios, political circles and financial markets. But what if the RBI was never meant to save the rupee in the first place?

NSE, BSE closed today for Bakri Id. Here's when stock markets reopenMarkets shut on 28 May for Bakri Id, with most trading segments closed. The break has left some investors checking timings, while commodity derivatives reopen in the evening and regular trading returns on 29 May.

Why do banks prefer lending to women more than men?For years, loans and borrowing have largely been seen as a man's domain. But banks are quietly noticing something interesting: women borrowers are often proving to be more reliable when it comes to repaying loans. So, are lenders beginning to trust women more with credit?

Need fairness: Supreme Court quashes CCI's Rs 202 crore penalty on AmazonThe Supreme Court set aside the CCI's Rs 202 crore penalty on Amazon and quashed orders suspending its Future Coupons deal. The ruling ends a five-year legal battle and underscores that regulators must act with legality, fairness and reasoned decision-making.

Hiranandani says India's affordable housing crisis is getting worse. Here's proofFor many aspiring homeowners, the biggest challenge today is no longer deciding where to buy a house, it is finding one that fits the budget. As affordable housing supply drops, experts warn that the problem is becoming harder to ignore.

US delegation to visit India from June 1–4 to finalise interim trade pactThe discussions are expected to focus on important areas such as market access, non-tariff measures, customs and trade facilitation, investment promotion and economic security alignment.

entertainment.headline: This 33-year-old Indian actor just beat Vijay, SRK on IMDb's most popular list Actor Yudhvir Ahlawat has drawn wide attention for playing Harpal in Netflix's Kartavya alongside Saif Ali Khan. Viewers mistook the 33-year-old for a child artiste, sending his IMDb popularity sharply upwards.

CM Vijay swaps black blazer for silk veshti-shirt at manager's housewarming Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay attended his manager Jagadish Palanisamy's housewarming in Chennai wearing a traditional silk veshti and shirt. The appearance drew attention as it was different from his usual black blazer style.

The Don 3 power game: You could be Bollywood's Dhurandhar, but we call the shots Ranveer Singh might be entering the best phase of his career yet, but what makes it compelling may also be what is quietly working against him in the industry. Conspiracy theories amid the Don 3 row or just a glimpse of Bollywood's insider reality?

Sigma: CM Vijay's son Jason Sanjay's debut directorial gets a release date The makers of Sigma have announced a worldwide theatrical release in July for Jason Sanjay's directorial debut starring Sundeep Kishan. The date puts the spotlight on Vijay's son as he launches a high-stakes heist entertainer still awaiting censor clearance.

It's official: Off Campus Season 2 is all about Dean and Allie Prime Video has set Allie Hayes and Dean Di Laurentis as the lead couple for Off Campus season 2. The new chapter adapts The Score and continues the series' plan of following a new connected couple each season.

Anupam Kher announces film on 'Shri Ram Bhoomi', Kerala Story 2 director joins Anupam Kher has kicked off work on Shri Ram Bhoomi, sharing a cheerful picture from the film's set with fans on Instagram. The actor described the project as a meaningful cinematic journey rooted in faith, truth and belonging.

Inside Ram Charan's meeting with PM Modi: Discussion on Peddi, idea of Viksit Bharat At a Delhi event, Ram Charan recalled Prime Minister Narendra Modi asking what his next film, Peddi is about. The actor used the exchange to underline the film's focus on 'Viksit Bharat', and the themese explored in it.

Yash's Toxic may be action-heavy, but Kiara Advani says it's about modern love While Toxic is packed with action and drama, Kiara Advani said the film's core lies in its take on modern love and relationships. The actor revealed that the Yash-starrer made her rethink the man-woman dynamic and pushed her creatively with its bilingual shoot.

The Great Grand Superhero review: Jackie Shroff's film is sweet, silly and nostalgicRelease Date:Fri May 29, 2026 The Great Grand Superhero movie review: The Great Grand Superhero, starring Jackie Shroff, is a sweet and nostalgic film that celebrates childhood imagination and the special bond with grandparents.

Actor Arjun Sarja calls Sanatan Dharma a way of life amid DMK's eradication remark At a film event, actor-drector Arjun Sarja said Sanatan Dharma is a way of life and outlined the values he believes it teaches. His remarks comes after DMK leader Udhayanidhi Stalin repeated his call for Sanatanam's eradication.

Even David Beckham and son Cruz couldn't keep their calm around Steve Carell Actor Steve Carell's surprise appearance on a commercial set left both David Beckham and his son Cruz starstruck. The humorous interaction quickly went viral, capturing the delight of fans as the trio shared laughs and posed for photos.

Respect for Indians not optional: Gurmeet Choudhary tears into viral US racism row Gurmeet Choudhary criticised racist remarks made to an Indian couple during a roadside interaction in the United States. His response added to wider online criticism of a video many users called discriminatory.

Jackie Shroff says he helped Salman Khan find work when he was not superstar Jackie Shroff, in a recent interview, recalled helping Salman Khan land his first film and revealed how he would carry the superstar's pictures to filmmakers while trying to get him good roles.

Passenger review: Andre Ovredal's road horror never fully reaches its destinationRelease Date:Fri May 29, 2026 Passenger movie review: Andre Ovredal's Passenger follows a newly engaged couple whose van-life trip turns sinister after they stop at a highway accident. The film finds its strongest note in Maddie's growing need for stability, even as the horror leans on familiar scares.

Veteran actor Sivakumar welcomes Vijay as Tamil Nadu CM, says voters wanted change At a Coimbatore event, veteran actor Sivakumar, Suriya and Karthi's father, welcomed Vijay's victory and said voters had chosen change. His remarks, alongside contrasting responses from actor Sathyaraj, highlight the Tamil film industry's varied reactions.

Ranveer Singh bets big on Pralay as Don 3 buzz and Meluha rumours swirl Amid the Don 3 din, Ranveer Singh is moving ahead with Pralay while emerging as the frontrunner for Aditya Dhar's next film. The slate looks bigger and costlier, even as writer Amish Tripathi has publicly denied any Meluha deal.

Amid Don 3 buzz, Salman Khan turns peacemaker for Ranveer, Farhan Salman Khan has reportedly reached out to Ranveer Singh and Farhan Akhtar amid the Don 3 row. The superstar seems to have acted as a mediator and attempted to resolve the matter amicably.

Wednesday star Emma Myers is hooked on Baahubali, 3 Idiots and we're not surprised Actor Emma Myers, in an interview, expressed her love for Indian cinema. She said 3 Idiots and Baahubali are among her favourite films.

Brad Pitt faces trademark suit from Malibu-based penis cream company Actor Brad Pitt's skincare label Beau Domaine is facing a trademark lawsuit from Malibu skincare brand. The case seeks damages and an injunction, putting the Pitt's skincare venture under legal scrutiny.

general news.headline: NEET, CBSE and Rs 1,000 crore mess: Expensive lesson that India didn't ask forThe NEET fallout and CBSE's Class 12 evaluation controversy have highlighted the escalating financial cost of exam failures. The episode has renewed questions over why authorities spend heavily on damage control instead of planning for prevention.
SC clears Vinesh Phogat for Asian Games trials, but with a stern messageSupreme Court allows wrestler Vinesh Phogat to participate in 2026 Asian Games trials

When Israel, India planned to strike Pak's Islamic bomb site; Why Indira said noAs Pakistan moved dangerously close to an "Islamic bomb" in the early 1980s, India and Israel explored a covert strike on Kahuta to prevent it from having a nuclear weapon. But why did PM Indira Gandhi call off the mission?

Siddaramaiah meets Rahul Gandhi with 'wishlist' day after stepping down as CMSources revealed that Siddaramaiah had pushed for key portfolios for his son, Yathindra, including medical education and backward classes, or alternatively industries or water resources -- a portfolio previously held by DK Shivakumar.

Conspiracy theories amid the Don 3 row or just a glimpse of Bollywood's insider reality?

Fleeing Bangladeshis reveal how they illegally crossed into IndiaIllegal Bangladeshi migrants, who are flocking to the Bangladesh border amid a crackdown in West Bengal, are describing how middlemen moved them into India and helped them in securing documents. Here's the entire trail, reconstructed from the admissions by the illegal immigrants themselves.

Claude AI bills getting out of control, company spends $500 mn in just one monthCompanies are embracing AI for work. But AI tools like Claude come at a cost, which can sometimes even shock you. As per reports, one company ended up spending $500,000,000 in a single month on Claude AI after it failed to set usage limits.

Air India, IndiGo cut 250 domestic flights; Mumbai, Delhi, Bengaluru worst hitThe capacity cuts by Air India and IndiGo will result in fewer flight options and higher fares this summer. Mumbai, Delhi and Bengaluru are the worst-affected cities, with many key routes seeing reduced frequencies during the June-August period.

Indian tourists dance to Chaiyya Chaiyya on Vietnam's Train Street, face backlashA video showed a group of Indian tourists dancing to the Bollywood song Chaiyya Chaiyya on Vietnam's famous Train Street. As the video went viral, the group faced considerable backlash, with many users arguing that such behaviour harms India's image abroad.

People are gaming Zomato and Swiggy with 'tiny orders'. We tried it ourselvesA new food-ordering method is becoming a hot topic online: place a tiny order on a delivery app (say, one roti), then ask the restaurant to combine it with a much larger offline order. The goal? To save money.

20 years on death row, Kerala man returns from Saudi Arabia as blood money is paidKerala native Abdul Rahim returned to his hometown on Eid after spending nearly 20 years on death row in a Saudi Arabian prison. He was pardoned following the payment of Rs 34 crore in blood money, which was raised through a crowdfunding campaign by Malayalis.

Why the Air Force may be roped in for NEET-UG re-examWith the NEET-UG re-test just weeks away, the Centre is considering using the Indian Air Force to transport the question papers under PMO monitoring. The move follows the May 3 paper leak fiasco. Experts said it signals a deep mistrust of the civilian machinery.

CM Vijay swaps black blazer for silk veshti-shirt at manager's housewarmingTamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay attended his manager Jagadish Palanisamy's housewarming in Chennai wearing a traditional silk veshti and shirt. The appearance drew attention as it was different from his usual black blazer style.

Siddaramaiah's quiet revenge on DKS and Rahul GandhiJust before stepping down as the Chief Minister of Karnataka, Siddaramaiah decided to accept a caste census report, pending since November 2025. With this, he is leaving a ticking bomb for his successor, DK Shivakumar, and the Congress's de facto chief, Rahul Gandhi.

Ban on smart glasses in IPL? BCCI announces tighter security protocolsIPL 2026: BCCI's Anti-Corruption Unit has banned smart glasses in IPL 2026 dressing rooms and restricted areas, citing security and integrity concerns. Players and staff must deposit the devices on match days.

12 dead, many critical after consuming toxic liquor in Pune; CM orders probeA suspected toxic liquor case has claimed 12 lives in Phugewadi and Hadapsar, with three others in critical condition. Investigators are probing whether methanol-laced liquor was responsible for the deaths.

What is the world's most expensive variety of mango?Tribal farmer Deba Padhiami has grown 17 Miyazaki mangoes in Odisha's Malkangiri after four years of care. The rare harvest has brought attention, security worries and a plea for help in finding premium buyers.

The Great Grand Superhero review: Jackie Shroff's film is sweet, silly and nostalgicRelease Date:Fri May 29, 2026The Great Grand Superhero movie review: The Great Grand Superhero, starring Jackie Shroff, is a sweet and nostalgic film that celebrates childhood imagination and the special bond with grandparents.

Rain today? Satellite images reveal what's bubbling in the skyAfter thunderstorms brought relief from extreme heat, Delhi-NCR and parts of North India are waiting to see if there is more rain in store. Here's the latest forecast.

In Congo's conflict zones, Ebola is spreading faster than doctors can trackA rare Ebola strain is spreading through Congo's war-hit mining regions, where violence, weak healthcare and the lack of a vaccine or rapid test are making the outbreak harder to control.

Tesla Model Y Premium RWD launched in India at Rs 50.89 lakhTesla has launched the 2026 Model Y Premium RWD in India at Rs 50.89 lakh, introducing a more affordable entry into its EV lineup with updated interiors, new technology and 500km range.

Indian-origin Shrey Parikh spelt this word to win US Spelling BeeShrey Parikh beat fellow Indian-origin finalist Ishaan Gupta in a tense lightning-round tiebreaker to win the Scripps National Spelling Bee, the United States' largest and longest-running educational competition.

Why RBI says India's biggest threat lies beyond its bordersFor all the talk around India's strong economic growth, resilient consumers and healthy banks, the RBI's latest assessment contains a clear message: the biggest risks to the country's growth story are increasingly coming from outside its borders.

Rajasthan woman, 22, pregnant with sixth child for another son; doctor raises alarmThe video was shared by Dr Pragya Tomar, a gynaecologist at Spandan Hospital in Baraut, who questioned the toll that repeated pregnancies can take on a woman's health when driven by a preference for male children.

sports.headline: Supreme Court allows wrestler Vinesh Phogat to participate in 2026 Asian Games trials Supreme Court allows wrestler Vinesh Phogat to participate in 2026 Asian Games trials

Thriller Night in Paris: Sabalenka responds to Djokovic with MJ-inspired dance moves French Open 2026: Novak Djokovic and Aryna Sabalenka have turned their French Open progress into a playful dance exchange in Paris. It has added levity to a tournament that has opened up further after Jannik Sinner's early exit.

Juan Cerundolo dumps Jannik Sinner out of French Open, elder brother left stunned French Open 2026: Francisco Cerundolo was left stunned in Paris as he learned mid-match that his younger brother Juan had produced a sensational five-set comeback to knock out World No.1 Jannik Sinner at Roland Garros.

Don't look at his age: Gavaskar believes that Sooryavanshi is ready for India call-up Sunil Gavaskar believes Vaibhav Suryavanshi is ready for India's T20 side, urging selectors to ignore his age and back the fearless batting prodigy after his stunning rise in cricket.

Ballon d'Or leaves Paris for London as award celebrates 70th anniversary The Ballon d'Or ceremony will leave Paris for London on October 26 in its 70th anniversary year. The move gives the awards a symbolic English marker, with Harry Kane, Ousmane Dembele and Aitana Bonmati shaping the contest.

Singapore Open Live Updates: PV Sindhu crashes out after losing 17-21, 14-21 Singapore Open 2026 Live Updates: PV Sindhu has crashed out of the tournament after An Se Young beat her 21-17, 21-14 in the quarterfinals on Friday, May 29.

Day 6 Schedule: Novak Djokovic, Iga Swiatek aim prequarters berth French Open 2026: Day 6 at Roland Garros 2026 features a stacked third-round schedule, with Novak Djokovic and Iga Swiatek headlining the action as they aim to reach the pre-quarterfinals in Paris. N Sriram Bajali and Yuki Bhambri will also be in action.

Ban on smart glasses in IPL? BCCI announces tighter security protocols IPL 2026: BCCI's Anti-Corruption Unit has banned smart glasses in IPL 2026 dressing rooms and restricted areas, citing security and integrity concerns. Players and staff must deposit the devices on match days.

Keep winning, then no one can deny me: Alex Perez eyes UFC title redemption Alex Perez heads into the Su Mudaerji fight in Macau insisting his only job is to keep winning. After injury, defeats and a contract cliff-edge, he believes another run will make the UFC take notice.

Norway Chess: Gukesh drops last after Carlsen loss, Praggnanandhaa stays second Magnus Carlsen dismantled D Gukesh with black to halt his own wobble at Norway Chess. The defeat left the world champion last, while Praggnanandhaa's resilience strengthened his firm hold on second place.

IPL Qualifier 2: Same same, but different as Gujarat and Rajasthan chase final spot IPL Qualifier 2 preview: Gujarat are bruised after a 92-run hammering. Rajasthan are flying on the back of a 15-year-old's 29-ball 97. One team arrives with momentum, the other with something to prove. Mullanpur decides who faces RCB in the final on Sunday.

Jemimah, Nandni, Yastika fire India to dominating win in T20I series opener vs EnglandINDW vs ENGW: India defeated England by 38 runs in the opening T20I of their tour, taking a 1-0 lead with strong all-round performances from Jemimah Rodrigues, Yastika Bhatia, and debutant Nandni Sharma.

It's official. Messi heads to the FIFA World Cup 2026 as defending championArgentina confirmed their 26-man World Cup roster, with Lionel Messi set to lead the defending champions. The 38-year-old makes his sixth appearance alongside 17 returning veterans from Qatar.

Ben Shelton ousted by Belgian debutant in 2nd round, shock exit for TsitsipasRoland Garros 2026: Raphael Collignon and Matteo Arnaldi produced two of the biggest upsets of the French Open, knocking out Ben Shelton and Stefanos Tsitsipas respectively. The giant-killers will now meet in the third round with a place in the second week at stake.

In Sooryavanshi's IPL, Sai Sudharsan bats on his own terms, undersungIPL 2026: In a tournament that worships power and rewards spectacle, Sai Sudharsan keeps finding boundaries the quiet way. The numbers are extraordinary. The recognition is not. Gujarat Titans would not have it any other way.

Justin Langer and the beautiful contradiction of Vaibhav SooryavanshiJustin Langer once asked his childhood hero Stephen Michael for a selfie. The second time he ever felt that urge came after watching 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi dismantle LSG in the IPL. He has now written a beautiful column about the most misunderstood word in sports - talent.

French Open: Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff made to work hard to reach round 3Aryna Sabalenka and Coco Gauff were pushed harder than expected before sealing straight-set wins in Paris to reach the third round of the French Open on Thursday, May 28.

Neymar's FIFA World Cup fairytale in threat, set to miss Brazil's openerNeymar has been ruled out of Brazil's next two friendlies after a grade two calf strain. The setback leaves his World Cup opener against Morocco in doubt and deepens Carlo Ancelotti's selection problem.

Hope the kid in Vaibhav Sooryavanshi never leaves: Sunil GavaskarFormer India cricketer Sunil Gavaskar has hoped that the kid in Vaibhav Sooryavanshi does not grow up too fast in the glitzy world of the Indian Premier League. Gavaskar revealed his conversations with the 15-year-old global star.

Nobody's a robot: Sinner set to take a long break after shock French Open exitWorld No.1 Jannik Sinner did not blame the intense Paris heat after getting knocked out of the French Open on Thursday. Speaking at the press conference, Sinner said that it was perhaps time to take a short break from the sport.

What did Gavaskar tell Vaibhav Sooryavanshi during viral feet-touching moment?Vaibhav Sooryavanshi touched Sunil Gavaskar's feet before the Rajasthan Royals' Eliminator against Sunrisers Hyderabad, and the Indian legend has now revealed what he actually told the young prodigy.

Premier League: Can Xabi Alonso spark a renaissance at Chelsea?Chelsea have turned to Xabi Alonso as the post-season Premier League churn gathers force. His arrival anchors a wider summer of exits, relegation fallout and the league's relentless money machine.

After creating history, 17-year-old stuffs head inside cooler to escape Paris heatwaveFrench Open: 17-year-old French youngster Moise Kouame became the youngest player since Rafael Nadal to reach the third round of the French Open. After winning a 5-set marathon, Kouane buried his head straight in the cooler right to escape the menacing heat of Paris.

Who is Juan Manuel Cerundolo? Argentine Nadal regen shocks Sinner at French OpenJuan Manuel Cerundolo came from 2-5 down in the third set to produce a stunning result against Jannik Sinner at Roland Garros. The win sent the Argentine into a first Grand Slam third round and opened up a possible meeting with brother Francisco.

Too hot to handle? Sinner cramps and crashes out in major French Open upset Live UpdatesJannik Sinner crashed out of the French Open after losing a 5-set encounter against unseeded Juan Manuel Cerundolo in Paris. The World No.1 struggled with dizziness, dehydration and movement issues in the intense heat after leading comfortably early in the match.

Stop Sooryavanshi age talk: GT assistant coach wants end to unwanted chatterParthiv Patel said Vaibhav Sooryavanshi should be judged for his batting, not his age. The Gujarat Titans assistant coach also kept his plans for the Rajasthan Royals opener under wraps ahead of Qualifier 2.

WFI challenges Delhi HC order allowing Vinesh Phogat into Asian Games trialsThe Wrestling Federation of India has moved the Supreme Court against the Delhi High Court's order permitting Vinesh Phogat to participate in the Asian Games 2026 selection trials, intensifying the federation's legal battle with the star wrestler over its revised eligibility policy.

Yashasvi Jaiswal must leave RR to break out of Sooryavanshi's shadow: RayuduAmbati Rayudu has said Yashasvi Jaiswal should leave Rajasthan Royals rather than remain in Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's shadow. Jaiswal has struggled for consistency this season with the bat as Sooryavanshi has gone ahead of him in terms of striking.

India Women turn training into water balloon war in fun session: VideoENG vs IND: India Women mixed fun with preparation ahead of the England T20Is, turning a unique water-balloon catching drill into a hilarious splash-filled battle during training.

Esha Singh wins Munich gold with 25m pistol world recordIndia's Esha Singh scripted history at the ISSF World Cup in Munich after clinching gold in the women's 25m pistol with a world record score. The 21-year-old outshot an elite field that included Olympic champion Yang Ji-in.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
